diagrams.title: Operator Overloading Decision Tree
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing when to use member vs friend function for operator overloading
elements: Built-in type (int, cout) -> Use Friend Function
Subscript ([]), Function (()), Arrow (->) -> Must be member
Return value for arithmetic operators returning new object
diagrams.purpose: Helps decide correct function type and return type for each operator

diagrams.title: Prefix vs Postfix Implementation
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of how prefix and postfix operators are implemented differently
elements: Memory: Prefix = 1 object, Postfix = 2 objects (original + copy)
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies implementation differences and efficiency considerations for increment operators

diagrams.title: Operator Overloading Rules Summary
diagrams.description: Visual summary of all rules and restrictions for operator overloading
elements: CAN: Overload for classes/structs (at least 1 user-defined operand)
CAN: Use member or friend functions
MUST: Return proper type (reference for chaining)
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for all operator overloading rules for exam preparation
